title: Per-task model routing
description: Maps role names to provider+model strings. `executor` is required (the catch-all that runs the §2.5 loop). `planner` and `critic` are well-known optional roles with specific §1 / §10 UI semantics (planner emits `plan_update` at task start; critic runs in the §10 side pane). Any **additional role key** is a free-form custom role — e.g., `documenter`, `web_trawler`, `architect`, `reviewer` — which the dispatcher routes to when a plan step carries a matching `role` tag (per §5 `PlanStep.constraints`). Lives at `<repo>/.atelier/routing.json` (per-repo, takes precedence) or `~/.atelier/routing.json` (global fallback). When absent, all roles default to the user's configured default executor; the critic is `null` (matches §10's 'off by default').

additionalProperties.description: Custom roles map to a provider+model or null (role disabled). Use this for fine-grained per-task routing: e.g., `documenter: ollama:llama3.2:3b` for low-cost doc-updates, `architect: anthropic:claude-opus-4-7` for deep design reasoning.

executor.description: Required. Runs the §2.5 agent loop turn-by-turn; emits the §2 envelope. Acts as the fallback for any plan step without a `role` tag.

planner.description: If null (default), the executor doubles as the planner. When set, the planner runs once at task start to emit a `plan_update` envelope; the executor takes over for the actual edits.

critic.description: If null (default), no critic runs — matches §10's 'background critic off by default'. When set, runs against each turn's output and emits an advisory envelope into a side pane; does not gate the §2.5 state machine.

model_ref.description: Format: '<provider>:<model_id>'. Provider names must match a registered §1 adapter (e.g., `anthropic`, `openai`, `bedrock`, `vertex`, `litellm`, `ollama`, `mlx`, `llamacpp`). Model IDs are provider-specific (e.g., `anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6`, `ollama:qwen2.5-coder:7b`, `bedrock:amazon.nova-pro-v1:0`, `vertex:gemini-2.0-flash-exp`).
